package main import ( "fmt" "html" "log" "net/http" "time" ) // HandleProductSearch handles product search requests with analytics func HandleProductSearch(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) { startTime := time.Now() // Parse query parameter keyword := r.URL.Query().Get("keyword") // Set content type w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "text/html; charset=utf-8") // Build HTML response htmlContent := fmt.Sprintf(` Product Search: %s

Search Results: %s

Showing products matching: %s

  • Product A - $19.99 (matches your search)
  • Product B - $29.99 (related to your search)
  • Product C - $14.99 (best match)

Search analytics:

  • Keyword searched: %s
  • Search processed in: %v
  • Results found: 3
  • Request method: %s
  • Timestamp: %s
`, html.EscapeString(keyword), html.EscapeString(keyword), html.EscapeString(keyword), html.EscapeString(keyword), time.Since(startTime), r.Method, time.Now().Format("2006-01-02 15:04:05"), ) // Write response w.Write([]byte(htmlContent)) // Log analytics log.Printf("SEARCH_ANALYTICS: keyword=%q method=%s duration=%v remote=%s", keyword, r.Method, time.Since(startTime), r.RemoteAddr) } func main() { // Register handler http.HandleFunc("/search", HandleProductSearch) http.HandleFunc("/", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) { http.Redirect(w, r, "/search?keyword=default", http.StatusFound) }) // Start server port := ":8080" fmt.Printf("Server starting on http://localhost%s\n", port) fmt.Println("Try: http://localhost:8080/search?keyword=laptop") log.Fatal(http.ListenAndServe(port, nil)) }
